title: Sphaeralcea dissecta
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea dissecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to North America. Sphaeralcea dissecta is commonly known as the desert marigold. This plant is a perennial herb that grows in arid and semiarid regions. Sphaeralcea dissecta belongs to the genus Sphaeralcea, which consists of several species of desert marigolds.
uses: Sphaeralcea dissecta, also known as desert marigold, has been traditionally used by Native Americans for its medicinal properties. The plant's flowers and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant's roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flowers are also used to make a tea that is said to have antiseptic and astringent properties. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Sphaeralcea dissecta for medicinal purposes.

These compounds are responsible for many of the plant's medicinal effects, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
